title: Contributing to imuGAP
text: Thank interest contributing imuGAP! document explains report issues, propose changes, expect CI pipeline open pull request.

title: Contributing to imuGAP
text: contributors expected respectful professional interactions — issues, pull requests, code reviews, discussions. Constructive feedback welcome; personal attacks dismissive language .

title: Contributing to imuGAP
text: Use bug report template opening new issue. asks description, minimal reprex, sessionInfo() output, operating system.

title: Contributing to imuGAP
text: Use feature request template. Describe use case , possible, feature fits existing model workflow (imuGAP() -> Stan sampling -> post-processing). can also open blank issue neither template fits.

title: Contributing to imuGAP
text: Fork & branch. Create feature branch main (e.g. fix/obs-population-check feature/predict-mode). Make changes. Follow style conventions . Test locally. Run R CMD check test suite pushing (see commands ). Open pull request main clear description changed .

title: Contributing to imuGAP
code: # Full rebuild + install (required after editing any .stan file) R CMD INSTALL --preclean .  # Regenerate documentation from roxygen comments Rscript -e 'devtools::document()'  # Run the test suite Rscript -e 'devtools::test()'  # Lint the package (config in .lintr) Rscript -e 'lintr::lint_package()'  # Build and check R CMD build . && R CMD check imuGAP_*.tar.gz

title: Contributing to imuGAP
text: R code linted lintr (configuration .lintr). R/stanmodels.R inst/analysis/ excluded linting generated standalone scripts, respectively. hand-edit generated files: R/stanmodels.R, src/stanExports_*.{cc,h}, configure, configure.win produced rstantools::rstan_config(). Stan model variants composed via #include toggling top-level .stan files — prefer toggling includes duplicating whole model files.

what: What Happens When You Open a PR
title: Contributing to imuGAP
text: Every pull request triggers two GitHub Actions workflows: workflows pass PR merged.

title: Contributing to imuGAP
text: something unclear, open issue comment existing one — happy help.

what: imuGAP: an Estimation Model for Measles Vaccination Coverage
title: imuGAP: an Estimation Model for Measles Vaccination Coverage
text:
name imuGAP stands “Immunity: Geographic & Age-based Projection”, name implies, package provides stan-based model estimating measles vaccination coverage location age cohort. core model represents target population life-long propensity vaccination; proportion, ϕ\phi, population unlikely vaccinate complementary proportion, 1−ϕ1 - \phi, likely vaccinate. population experiences vaccination rate, λ\lambda, model time eras, according vaccination eligibility schedule, ν
u. core parameters can vary time location, user-specifiable way. Focusing just core model element, imagine particular population location ii cohort aa (aa denotes start time period group born). cohort now age tt, vaccine schedule first dose ν(t)
u(t), expected fraction group least one dose : P(≥1 dose)=(1−ϕi,)(1−exp{−∫atλi,(s)ν(s)ds}) P(\ge	extrm{1 dose}) = \left(1 - \phi_{, }ight) \left(1 - \exp\left\{-\int_a^{t} \lambda_{, }(s)
u(s) d	extrm{s}ight\}ight) say, representing vaccination coverage via survival model. Leaving aside “unlikely” population

title: Authors and Citation
text: Carl Pearson. Author, maintainer. Claire Perrin Smith. Author. Kelly Zhen. Contributor. Weston Voglesonger. Contributor. Joshua Chen. Contributor. Minjae Kung. Contributor.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Pearson C, Smith C (2026). imuGAP: Estimate Measles Vaccine Coverage. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://accidda.github.io/imuGAP/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {imuGAP: Estimate Measles Vaccine Coverage},   author = {Carl Pearson and Claire Perrin Smith},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://accidda.github.io/imuGAP/}, }

title: Estimate Measles Vaccine Coverage
text: imuGAP provides fitting imputation / prediction tool particular process model vaccination uptake. tool provides flexible trends relationships elements process.

title: Estimate Measles Vaccine Coverage
text: imuGAP sampler fits two model effects: lifetime tendency towards vaccination time varying force vaccination. fit effects, model evaluates observations may combine many populations. model distinguishes populations place, birth timing, age. model organizes places hierarchy, example state contains counties turn contain cities. effects particular place combine impact enclosing locations.

title: Estimate Measles Vaccine Coverage
text: Requires R >= 4.1.0 C++ toolchain (Stan model compilation).

title: Estimate Measles Vaccine Coverage
text: installing package, set command-line interface: creates symlink ~/.local/bin/imugap. Make sure ~/.local/bin PATH. need run install_cli() installing updating package; refreshes symlink points current install.

title: Estimate Measles Vaccine Coverage
text: output_dir defaults input_dir specified. Output fit.rds file containing raw stanfit object.
code: # Show help imugap -h  # Validate input data (dry run, no model fitting) imugap -h <input_dir>  # Run model fitting imugap <input_dir> [output_dir]

title: Estimate Measles Vaccine Coverage
text: input_dir must contain three files (CSV RDS):

title: Canonicalize Location Data — canonicalize_locations
text: Canonicalize Location Data

title: Canonicalize Location Data — canonicalize_locations
text: locations [data.frame()], columns loc_id parent_id, type. See Details restrictions.

title: Canonicalize Location Data — canonicalize_locations
text: data.table, : loc_id, parent_id columns originally supplied, possibly reordered loc_c_id, loc_cp_id columns, canonicalized id/parent_id columns, representing order used sampler layer column, integer 1 (root), 2 (root children), 3 (grandchildren), &c layer_bound column, integer starting 1 layer. provides index slice information used stan model.

title: Canonicalize Location Data — canonicalize_locations
text: [imuGAP()] sampler works hierarchical model locations, must provided structure. method checks location structure validity, returns canonical version including layer membership. valid structure : unique root, cycles, duplicate loc_ids Users may explicitly identify root loc_id providing row parent_id equal NA. Otherwise, parent_id appear loc_id treated root. input valid, method create canonicalized version. version, ids run 1:N, N number distinct ids. order determined layer order, position parent within layer, "natural" order (.e., whatever base R sort() yields).

title: Canonicalize Observation Data — canonicalize_observations
text: Canonicalize Observation Data

title: Canonicalize Observation Data — canonicalize_observations
text: observations [data.frame()], observed data, least three columns: obs_id column; type, long unique, non-NA `positive“ column; non-negative integers, observed number vaccinated individuals sample_n column; positive integers, number individuals sampled, must greater equal "positive" optionally, censored column; numeric, NA (uncensored) 1 (right-censored); present, assumed NA drop_extra logical scalar; drop extraneous columns? (default: yes)

title: Canonicalize Observation Data — canonicalize_observations
text: canonical observation object, [data.table()] : obs_c_id column, integer sequence 1; order observations passed estimation original obs_id column, possibly reordered positive sample_n columns, possibly reordered "censored" column; NA, present original observations argument

title: Canonicalize Observation Data — canonicalize_observations
text: observations object documents observations used fit model. Conceptually, row represents observation vaccination status within population. population need uniform (see [canonicalize_populations()]) concerning single cohort time: observation generally best available resolution data. resolution can vary across rows. [imuGAP()] sampler uses information resolutions automatically figure compare latent process model different observations. optional censored column: model supports vaccination status indicators vaccine specific well represent individual set vaccines (including target vaccine). specific coverage target vaccine right-censored latter case: full-set-coverage minimum coverage target. least data censored, must supply censored column correctly estimate coverage. Mark uncensored observations NA, right-censored observations $1$. Note $0$ valid value time; preserving potential future support left-censoring.

title: Check observation meta data object — canonicalize_populations
text: populations [data.frame()], observation meta data, columns obs_id, type; observation row concerns (.e. id shared observations data object) `loc_id“, type; location row concerns (.e. id shared locations data object) dose, non-zero, positive integer (1, 2, ...); dose row concerns cohort, positive integer; cohort location row concerns age, positive integer; age cohort row concerns weight, numeric, (0, 1); relative contribution row observation Note multiple rows may concern observation, meaning populations different cohorts, locations, ages may pooled observation observations [data.frame()], observed data, least three columns: obs_id column; type, long unique, non-NA `positive“ column; non-negative integers, observed number vaccinated individuals sample_n column; positive integers, number individuals sampled, must greater equal "positive" optionally, censored column; numeric, NA (uncensored) 1 (right-censored); present, assumed NA locations [data.frame()], columns loc_id parent_id, type. See Details restrictions. max_cohort present, maximum cohort present? max_age present, maximum age present? max_dose maximum dose number allow (default: 2L)

title: Check observation meta data object — canonicalize_populations
text: canonical populations object, mirroring input populations, following updates: obs_c_id, observation id row concerns, canonicalized match canonical observation ids loc_c_id, location id row concerns, canonicalized match reordered obs_c_id order

title: Check observation meta data object — canonicalize_populations
text: method validates meta-data associated observations, well converting meta-data use canonical id formats. Regarding "cohorts" "ages": counted 1, 1 "unit". can imagine units whatever resolution appropriate data: months, quarters, years, etc. long used consistently, estimation work, take unit meaning used input.

title: imuGAP Model Options — imugap_options
code: imugap_options() #> $df #> [1] 5 #>  #> $dose_schedule #> [1] 1 4 #>  #> $object #> S4 class stanmodel 'impute_school_coverage_process_v6' coded as follows: #> functions { #>   // a function to convert lower bounds l_1, 1_2, ... 1_n #>   // to (lower, upper) pairs (l_1, l_2-1), (l_2, l_3-1), ... #>   array[,] int bounds_to_range(array[] int lowers, int ub) { #>     int size_bounds = size(lowers); #>     if (lowers[size_bounds] > ub) { #>         print("Upper bound, ", ub, " is less than last lower bound, ", lowers[size_bounds]); #>     } #>     array[size_bounds] int uppers; #>     for (i in 1:(size_bounds - 1)) { #>         uppers[i] = lowers[i+1] - 1; #>     } #>     uppers[size_bounds] = ub; #>     return { lowers, uppers }; #>   } #>   // create a matrix, each column multiplied by corresponding row entry #>   matrix element_mult_expand(vector colv, row_vector rowv) { #>     int nrows = size(colv), ncols = size(rowv); #>     matrix[nrows, ncols] result; #>     for (i in 1:nrows) { #>         result[i,] = rowv * colv[i]; #>     } #>     return result; #>   } #>   // Sequential diff #>   vector diff(vector obj) { #>     int sz = size(obj); #>     return obj[2:] - obj[:(sz-1)]; #>   } #>   row_vector diff(row_vector obj) { #>     int sz = size(obj); #>     return obj[2:] - obj[:(sz-1)]; #>   } #>   row_vector colsum(matrix obj) { #>     int ncols = cols(obj); #>     row_vector[ncols] res; #>     for (i in 1:ncols) { #>         res[i] = sum(obj[, i]); #>     } #>     return res; #>   } #>   vector rowsum(matrix obj) { #>     int nrows = rows(obj); #>     vector[nrows] res; #>     for (i in 1:nrows) { #>         res[i] = sum(obj[i, ]); #>     } #>     return res; #>   } #> vector unrolled_dose(int n_yr, int n_doses, matrix dose_sched, vector lambda_raw, real epsilon_p) { #>   // assert: dose_sched is n_yr x n_doses #>   // assert: lambda_raw is n_doses x 1 (alt: would be n_doses x n_time) #>   vector[n_doses] lambda = exp(lambda_raw); #>   // the unconditional cdfs, given FoV + whatever remains; normalization factors #>   matrix[n_yr, n_doses] dXcdf, dXpdf, normdXpdf; #>   vector[n_doses] rem; #>   for (dose in 1:n_doses) { #>     dXcdf[, dose] = 1 - exp(-cumulative_sum(dose_sched[, dose] * lambda[dose])); #>     rem[dose] = 1 - dXcdf[n_yr, dose]; #>     dXpdf[1, dose] = dXcdf[1, dose]; #>     dXpdf[2:,dose] = diff(dXcdf[, dose]); #>     normdXpdf[, dose] = reverse(cumulative_sum(reverse(dXpdf[, dose]))) + rem[dose]; #>   } #>   matrix[n_yr, n_doses] conditional_dXpdf = rep_matrix(0, n_yr, n_doses), conditional_dXcdf; #>   conditional_dXpdf[, 1] = dXpdf[, 1]; #>   conditional_dXcdf[, 1] = dXcdf[, 1]; #>   for (dose in 2:n_doses) { #>     int prev_dose = dose - 1; #>     // conditional probability => probability got dose n-1 at some time, then probability got dose n at later times #>     for (ly in 1:n_yr) { #>       if (normdXpdf[ly, dose] < epsilon_p) break; // if the remaining weight is negligible, avoid division by zero #>       conditional_dXpdf[ly:, dose] += conditional_dXpdf[ly, prev_dose] * dXpdf[ly:, dose] / normdXpdf[ly, dose]; #>     } #>     conditional_dXcdf[, dose] = cumulative_sum(conditional_dXpdf[, dose]); #>   } #>   // TODO check unrolling of cdfs - should be dose 1 all years, then dose 2 all years, etc #>   vector[n_doses * n_yr] unrolled_dose_cdf = to_vector(conditional_dXcdf); #>   return unrolled_dose_cdf; #> } #> } #> // need meta info: #> //  which cohorts, which places, which years of life #> //  => assume each measurement is independent, not progress of some matched individuals #> data { #> // #include data/shared_stateonly.stan #>   // STRUCTURAL DEFINITIONS #>   int<lower=1> n_yr; // number of years to model for each cohort - should be at least year of oldest observation #>   int<lower=1> n_cohort; // number of birth year cohorts #>   int<lower=1> n_sch; // number of schools #>  #>   // maps schools to county #>   int<lower=1> n_cnty; #>   array[n_cnty] int<lower=1, upper=n_sch> cnty_bounds; // which schools indices start each county #>   // dose schedules #>   int<lower=1> n_doses; #>   matrix<lower=0, upper=1>[n_yr, n_doses] dose_sched; #>   // DATA DEFINITIONS #>   int<lower=1> n_obs; #>   int<lower=0> y_obs[n_obs]; #>   int<lower=0> y_smp[n_obs]; #>   // have school id ranges for observations & for doses; school id 0 == statewide? #>   // int obs_sch_id_bounds[n_obs]; #>   int<lower=n_obs> n_weights; #>   array[n_obs] int<lower=1, upper=n_weights> obs_to_weights_bounds; // each entry is the start of the range #>   int<lower=1,upper=n_sch + n_cnty + 1> weights_school[n_weights]; #>   int<lower=1,upper=n_cohort> weights_cohort[n_weights]; #>   int<lower=1,upper=n_yr> weights_life_year[n_weights]; #>   int<lower=1,upper=n_doses> weights_dose[n_weights]; #>   vector<lower=0,upper=1>[n_weights] weights; // contribution of this (school, cohort, year, dose) to an observation #>   // run mode: 0 = estimation, 1 = prediction #>   int<lower=0, upper=1> predict_mode; #>   // TODO: calculate these in stan? #>   // https://spinkney.github.io/helpful_stan_functions/group__splines.html #>   // state-level basis spline #>   int k_bs; // number of bspline basis functions #>   matrix[n_cohort, k_bs] bs; // basis functions #> # observations may be right-censored #> # observation data is assumed ordered uncensored, then right censored #> # so n_uncensored_obs == n_obs, all observations are uncensored #> # number of uncensored observations #> int<lower = 0, upper = n_obs> n_uncensored_obs; #> } #> transformed data { #>    // #include transformed_data/stateonly.stan #>    // #include transformed_data/fixed_raw_lambda.stan #>    // #include transformed_data/fixed_logit_phi.stan #> real epsilon_p = 1e-10; #>   // convert to lookup spans for convenience #>   array[2, n_cnty] int cnty_map = bounds_to_range(cnty_bounds, n_sch); #>   array[2, n_obs] int obs_map = bounds_to_range(obs_to_weights_bounds, n_weights); #>  #>   // Equivalent 1:n_cohort, for time trends #>   vector[n_cohort] cohort_shift_counter = linspaced_vector(n_cohort, 1, n_cohort); #>   int<lower=1> phi_lookup[n_weights]; #>   int<lower=1> cdf_lookup[n_weights]; #>   // because integer arrays don't support broadcasting ... #>   // unroll phi and cdf objects to support vectorization #>   for (weight_i in 1:n_weights) { #>     // phi ordered by school then cohort #>     phi_lookup[weight_i] = weights_cohort[weight_i] + (weights_school[weight_i] - 1) * n_cohort; #>     // ordered by dose then life year #>     cdf_lookup[weight_i] = weights_life_year[weight_i] + (weights_dose[weight_i] - 1) * n_yr; #>   } #> int<lower=-1> y_obs_trans[n_obs]; #> for(i in 1:n_obs) { #>   y_obs_trans[i] = y_obs[i] - 1; #> } #> } #> parameters { #>   // bases spline coeficcients #>   vector[k_bs] beta_bs; // spline betas #>   // #include parameters/constant_phi.stan #>   // #include parameters/cnty_sch_linear.stan #>   // offsets #>   real<lower=0> sigma_cnty; #>   row_vector<multiplier=sigma_cnty>[n_cnty] off_cnty; #>   real<lower=0> sigma_sch; #>   row_vector<multiplier=sigma_sch>[n_sch] off_sch; #>   // Vaccination uptake rate #>   vector[n_doses] lambda_raw; #> } #> transformed parameters { #>   // state-level phi by year, constructed from beta spline #>   // Construct state-level trend from basis functions #>   // yields logit_phi_st #> vector[n_cohort] logit_phi_st = bs*beta_bs; #>   // #include transformed_parameters/constant_phi.stan #>   // #include transformed_parameters/cnty_sch_linear.stan #>   row_vector[n_sch] shift = off_sch; #>   for (c in 1:n_cnty) { #>     shift[cnty_map[1,c]:cnty_map[2,c]] += off_cnty[c]; // add county offsets to relevant school offsets #>   } #>  #>   // school-level phi by year, converted to column major vector #>   // so it's school n, cohort 1 => n_cohort; school n+1, ... #>   // TODO double check mapping from matrix to vector #>   vector<lower=0, upper=1>[(1+ n_cnty + n_sch) * n_cohort] phi = append_row(append_row( #>     inv_logit(logit_phi_st), #>     to_vector(inv_logit( #>       rep_matrix(logit_phi_st, n_cnty) + // apply state terms for every cohort across counties => n_cohort x 1 => n_cohort x n_cnty #>       rep_matrix(off_cnty, n_cohort) // apply base school terms across every cohort => 1 x n_cnty => n_cohort x n_cnty #>     ))), #>     to_vector(inv_logit( #>       rep_matrix(logit_phi_st, n_sch) + // apply state terms for every cohort across schools => n_cohort x 1 => n_cohort x n_sch #>       rep_matrix(shift, n_cohort) // apply base school terms across every cohort => 1 x n_sch => n_cohort x n_sch #>     )) // n_cohort x n_sch => n_cohort * n_sch #>   ); #>   vector<lower=0, upper=1>[n_doses * n_yr] unrolled_dose_probs = unrolled_dose(n_yr, n_doses, dose_sched, lambda_raw, epsilon_p); #>   // state-wide obs => school_id == n_sch + 1; TODO check vectorization flattening order #>   vector<lower=0,upper=1>[n_weights] weighted = (1 - phi[phi_lookup]) .* unrolled_dose_probs[cdf_lookup] .* weights; #>   // convert conditional_dXcdf to p_obs, according to the observation weights #>   vector<lower=0,upper=1>[n_obs] p_obs; #>   // the observed probability of having previously gotten dose X is: #>   //  - the weighted combination of relevant cohorts X relevant life years X relevant schools #>   //  - a cohort X year X school probability is #>   //    conditional_dXcdf[dose, cohort_life_year] * (1 - phi[school, cohort]) #>   for (obs_i in 1:n_obs) { #>     p_obs[obs_i] = sum(weighted[obs_map[1,obs_i]:obs_map[2,obs_i]]); #>   } #> } #> model { #>   if (!predict_mode) { #>     // PRIORS - spline coefficients #>     beta_bs ~ normal(0, 10); #>     // #include model/constant_phi.stan #>     // PRIOR - lambda; relatively strong prior belief that ~95% coverage achieved in a year #>     // mean of 3 => 1 - exp(-3*1) == ~ 0.95 #>     lambda_raw ~ normal(log(3), 1); #>     // Offsets - non-centered parameterization to speed up #>     sigma_cnty ~ cauchy(0, 1); #>     off_cnty ~ normal(0, sigma_cnty); #>     sigma_sch ~ cauchy(0, 2); #>     off_sch ~ normal(0, sigma_sch); #>     // #include model/cnty_sch_linear.stan #> if (n_uncensored_obs < n_obs) { # at least some censored observations #>     # p_s => 1 - p_s = p_f :: probability of at least this many successes => #>     #                         probability of less than this many failures #>     if (n_uncensored_obs > 0) { # at least some uncensored observations #>         target += binomial_lpmf(y_obs[:n_uncensored_obs] | y_smp[:n_uncensored_obs], p_obs[:n_uncensored_obs]); #>     } #>     target += binomial_lcdf(y_obs[(n_uncensored_obs+1):] | y_smp[(n_uncensored_obs+1):], 1 - p_obs[(n_uncensored_obs+1):]); #> } else { # all uncensored observations #>     y_obs ~ binomial(y_smp, p_obs); # vectorized #> } #>   } #> } #> generated quantities { #> vector<lower=0, upper=1>[n_doses * n_yr] unrolled_dose_probs_gen = unrolled_dose(n_yr, n_doses, dose_sched, lambda_raw, epsilon_p); #> // state-wide obs => school_id == n_sch + 1; TODO check vectorization flattening order #> vector<lower=0,upper=1>[n_weights] weighted_gen = (1 - phi[phi_lookup]) .* unrolled_dose_probs[cdf_lookup] .* weights; #> // convert conditional_dXcdf to p_obs, according to the observation weights #> vector<lower=0,upper=1>[n_obs] p_gen; #> // the observed probability of having previously gotten dose X is: #> //  - the weighted combination of relevant cohorts X relevant life years X relevant schools #> //  - a cohort X year X school probability is #> //    conditional_dXcdf[dose, cohort_life_year] * (1 - phi[school, cohort]) #> for (obs_i in 1:n_obs) { #>   p_gen[obs_i] = sum(weighted[obs_map[1,obs_i]:obs_map[2,obs_i]]); #> } #> } #> // This model represents vaccination as a discrete step, fixed hazard process #> // therefore X(t) => X(t + deltaT) = X(t)*exp(-hazard*deltaT) #> // which means the X-out flow == X(t)*(1-exp(-hazard*deltaT)) #> // #> // for an arbitrary number of hazards & deltaTs, we simply sum: #> // net outflow: X(t)*(1-exp(-sum(hazard*deltaT))) #> // #> // This model represents an overall hazard, shared across birth cohorts and #> // vaccine doses. Additionally, birth cohorts have a propensity to vaccinate, #> // which is fixed at birth and then applies throughout life. #> // #> // deltaT captures both time passage + eligibility. deltaT in the fitting is #> // always == 1, but note that this is unitless - the data define what time means #> // e.g. if birth cohorts are based on years, then deltaT is years, and a weight #> // of 1 means eligible for a whole year, .25 eligible for a quarter, etc #> // #> // the hazard is constant for any given discrete time step, but can vary between #> // time steps. the hazard time is denoted in absolute model time. however, #> // cohort time is relative. so: #> // hazard time == 1: one deltaT has passed, absolute time is 1 #> // cohort 1, time 1: one deltaT has passed => cohort 1 is 1 deltaT old, #> //   absolute time is also 1 (cohort 1 is the first cohort) #> // cohort 2, time 2: two deltaT have passed => cohort 2 is 2 deltaT old, but #> //   cohort 1 is 3 deltaT old, and absolute time is 3 #> // generally: #> // cohort n, time t: experienced t deltaTs; those deltaT corresponded to absolute #> //   times n, n+1, ..., n+t #> // #> // we assume each cohort has the same doses schedule, so the same deltaT weights #> // #> // so for cohort n, fraction of (non-phi/vaccinators) with 1+ doses at age 1:t: #> //   d1cdf = 1 - exp(-cumsum(weight[1:t] .* lambda[n:t])) #> // #> // and the incidence of new dose 1s is: #> // #> //   c(d1cdf[1], diff(d1cdf)) #> // #> // for subsequent doses, the hazard model is the same, but now is conditional on #> // having received the first dose; thus the incremental hazard is still #> //   d2cdf = 1 - exp(-cumsum(weight2[1:t] .* lambda[n:t])) #> //   (note the different eligibility mask, weight2; assert weight2 strictly less than weight1) #> // but only the fraction having dose 1 experience it. #> // if we think about this in terms of newly-dose-1-in-deltaT-x: #> //   we can reframe in these terms: #> //   define d2pdf = c(d2cdf[1], diff(d2cdf)); pgtt = 1-d2cdf[t] (probably event has not occured by t) #> //   probability of dose 2 in year (x+1):t => d2pdf[(x+1):t]/sum(d2pdf[x+1:t] + pggt) #> //   we can then assign each incremental incidence of dose 1 in deltaT x to getting #> //   second dose in deltaT x+1:t (or not getting it by t) #> // #> // this can be built-up as a matrix once per cohort #> //  1. calculate d2cdf, d2pdf, pgtt as above, for t == max cohort "age" #> //  2. calculate remd2pdf = rev(cumsum(rev(d2pdf))) + pgtt #> //  3. d2contrib = #> //    upper_triangle_1s * colwise element multiplication of d2pdf / rowwise remd2pdf * rowwise d2incidence => column sums => proportion in d2  #>  imugap_options(dose_schedule = c(1, 3)) #> $df #> [1] 5 #>  #> $dose_schedule #> [1] 1 3 #>  #> $object #> S4 class stanmodel 'impute_school_coverage_process_v6' coded as follows: #> functions { #>   // a function to convert lower bounds l_1, 1_2, ... 1_n #>   // to (lower, upper) pairs (l_1, l_2-1), (l_2, l_3-1), ... #>   array[,] int bounds_to_range(array[] int lowers, int ub) { #>     int size_bounds = size(lowers); #>     if (lowers[size_bounds] > ub) { #>         print("Upper bound, ", ub, " is less than last lower bound, ", lowers[size_bounds]); #>     } #>     array[size_bounds] int uppers; #>     for (i in 1:(size_bounds - 1)) { #>         uppers[i] = lowers[i+1] - 1; #>     } #>     uppers[size_bounds] = ub; #>     return { lowers, uppers }; #>   } #>   // create a matrix, each column multiplied by corresponding row entry #>   matrix element_mult_expand(vector colv, row_vector rowv) { #>     int nrows = size(colv), ncols = size(rowv); #>     matrix[nrows, ncols] result; #>     for (i in 1:nrows) { #>         result[i,] = rowv * colv[i]; #>     } #>     return result; #>   } #>   // Sequential diff #>   vector diff(vector obj) { #>     int sz = size(obj); #>     return obj[2:] - obj[:(sz-1)]; #>   } #>   row_vector diff(row_vector obj) { #>     int sz = size(obj); #>     return obj[2:] - obj[:(sz-1)]; #>   } #>   row_vector colsum(matrix obj) { #>     int ncols = cols(obj); #>     row_vector[ncols] res; #>     for (i in 1:ncols) { #>         res[i] = sum(obj[, i]); #>     } #>     return res; #>   } #>   vector rowsum(matrix obj) { #>     int nrows = rows(obj); #>     vector[nrows] res; #>     for (i in 1:nrows) { #>         res[i] = sum(obj[i, ]); #>     } #>     return res; #>   } #> vector unrolled_dose(int n_yr, int n_doses, matrix dose_sched, vector lambda_raw, real epsilon_p) { #>   // assert: dose_sched is n_yr x n_doses #>   // assert: lambda_raw is n_doses x 1 (alt: would be n_doses x n_time) #>   vector[n_doses] lambda = exp(lambda_raw); // add county offsets to relevant school offsets #>   } #>  #>   // school-level phi by year, converted to column major vector #>   // so it's school n, cohort 1 => n_cohort; school n+1, ... #>   // TODO double check mapping from matrix to vector #>   vector<lower=0, upper=1>[(1+ n_cnty + n_sch) * n_cohort] phi = append_row(append_row( #>     inv_logit(logit_phi_st), #>     to_vector(inv_logit( #>       rep_matrix(logit_phi_st, n_cnty) + // apply state terms for every cohort across counties => n_cohort x 1 => n_cohort x n_cnty #>       rep_matrix(off_cnty, n_cohort) // apply base school terms across every cohort => 1 x n_cnty => n_cohort x n_cnty #>     ))), #>     to_vector(inv_logit( #>       rep_matrix(logit_phi_st, n_sch) + // apply state terms for every cohort across schools => n_cohort x 1 => n_cohort x n_sch #>       rep_matrix(shift, n_cohort) // apply base school terms across every cohort => 1 x n_sch => n_cohort x n_sch #>     )) // n_cohort x n_sch => n_cohort * n_sch #>   ); #>   vector<lower=0, upper=1>[n_doses * n_yr] unrolled_dose_probs = unrolled_dose(n_yr, n_doses, dose_sched, lambda_raw, epsilon_p); #>   // state-wide obs => school_id == n_sch + 1; TODO check vectorization flattening order #>   vector<lower=0,upper=1>[n_weights] weighted = (1 - phi[phi_lookup]) .* unrolled_dose_probs[cdf_lookup] .* weights; #>   // convert conditional_dXcdf to p_obs, according to the observation weights #>   vector<lower=0,upper=1>[n_obs] p_obs; #>   // the observed probability of having previously gotten dose X is: #>   //  - the weighted combination of relevant cohorts X relevant life years X relevant schools #>   //  - a cohort X year X school probability is #>   //    conditional_dXcdf[dose, cohort_life_year] * (1 - phi[school, cohort]) #>   for (obs_i in 1:n_obs) { #>     p_obs[obs_i] = sum(weighted[obs_map[1,obs_i]:obs_map[2,obs_i]]); #>   } #> } #> model { #>   if (!predict_mode) { #>     // PRIORS - spline coefficients #>     beta_bs ~ normal(0, 10); #>     // #include model/constant_phi.stan #>     // PRIOR - lambda; relatively strong prior belief that ~95% coverage achieved in a year #>     // mean of 3 => 1 - exp(-3*1) == ~ 0.95 #>     lambda_raw ~ normal(log(3), 1); #>     // Offsets - non-centered parameterization to speed up #>     sigma_cnty ~ cauchy(0, 1); #>     off_cnty ~ normal(0, sigma_cnty); #>     sigma_sch ~ cauchy(0, 2); #>     off_sch ~ normal(0, sigma_sch); #>     // #include model/cnty_sch_linear.stan #> if (n_uncensored_obs < n_obs) { # at least some censored observations #>     # p_s => 1 - p_s = p_f :: probability of at least this many successes => #>     #                         probability of less than this many failures #>     if (n_uncensored_obs > 0) { # at least some uncensored observations #>         target += binomial_lpmf(y_obs[:n_uncensored_obs] | y_smp[:n_uncensored_obs], p_obs[:n_uncensored_obs]); #>     } #>     target += binomial_lcdf(y_obs[(n_uncensored_obs+1):] | y_smp[(n_uncensored_obs+1):], 1 - p_obs[(n_uncensored_obs+1):]); #> } else { # all uncensored observations #>     y_obs ~ binomial(y_smp, p_obs); # vectorized #> } #>   } #> } #> generated quantities { #> vector<lower=0, upper=1>[n_doses * n_yr] unrolled_dose_probs_gen = unrolled_dose(n_yr, n_doses, dose_sched, lambda_raw, epsilon_p); #> // state-wide obs => school_id == n_sch + 1; TODO check vectorization flattening order #> vector<lower=0,upper=1>[n_weights] weighted_gen = (1 - phi[phi_lookup]) .* unrolled_dose_probs[cdf_lookup] .* weights; #> // convert conditional_dXcdf to p_obs, according to the observation weights #> vector<lower=0,upper=1>[n_obs] p_gen; #> // the observed probability of having previously gotten dose X is: #> //  - the weighted combination of relevant cohorts X relevant life years X relevant schools #> //  - a cohort X year X school probability is #> //    conditional_dXcdf[dose, cohort_life_year] * (1 - phi[school, cohort]) #> for (obs_i in 1:n_obs) { #>   p_gen[obs_i] = sum(weighted[obs_map[1,obs_i]:obs_map[2,obs_i]]); #> } #> } #> // This model represents vaccination as a discrete step, fixed hazard process #> // therefore X(t) => X(t + deltaT) = X(t)*exp(-hazard*deltaT) #> // which means the X-out flow == X(t)*(1-exp(-hazard*deltaT)) #> // #> // for an arbitrary number of hazards & deltaTs, we simply sum: #> // net outflow: X(t)*(1-exp(-sum(hazard*deltaT))) #> // #> // This model represents an overall hazard, shared across birth cohorts and #> // vaccine doses. Additionally, birth cohorts have a propensity to vaccinate, #> // which is fixed at birth and then applies throughout life. #> // #> // deltaT captures both time passage + eligibility. deltaT in the fitting is #> // always == 1, but note that this is unitless - the data define what time means #> // e.g. if birth cohorts are based on years, then deltaT is years, and a weight #> // of 1 means eligible for a whole year, .25 eligible for a quarter, etc #> // #> // the hazard is constant for any given discrete time step, but can vary between #> // time steps. the hazard time is denoted in absolute model time. however, #> // cohort time is relative. so: #> // hazard time == 1: one deltaT has passed, absolute time is 1 #> // cohort 1, time 1: one deltaT has passed => cohort 1 is 1 deltaT old, #> //   absolute time is also 1 (cohort 1 is the first cohort) #> // cohort 2, time 2: two deltaT have passed => cohort 2 is 2 deltaT old, but #> //   cohort 1 is 3 deltaT old, and absolute time is 3 #> // generally: #> // cohort n, time t: experienced t deltaTs; those deltaT corresponded to absolute #> //   times n, n+1, ..., n+t #> // #> // we assume each cohort has the same doses schedule, so the same deltaT weights #> // #> // so for cohort n, fraction of (non-phi/vaccinators) with 1+ doses at age 1:t: #> //   d1cdf = 1 - exp(-cumsum(weight[1:t] .* lambda[n:t])) #> // #> // and the incidence of new dose 1s is: #> // #> //   c(d1cdf[1], diff(d1cdf)) #> // #> // for subsequent doses, the hazard model is the same, but now is conditional on #> // having received the first dose; thus the incremental hazard is still #> //   d2cdf = 1 - exp(-cumsum(weight2[1:t] .* lambda[n:t])) #> //   (note the different eligibility mask, weight2; assert weight2 strictly less than weight1) #> // but only the fraction having dose 1 experience it. #> // if we think about this in terms of newly-dose-1-in-deltaT-x: #> //   we can reframe in these terms: #> //   define d2pdf = c(d2cdf[1], diff(d2cdf)); pgtt = 1-d2cdf[t] (probably event has not occured by t) #> //   probability of dose 2 in year (x+1):t => d2pdf[(x+1):t]/sum(d2pdf[x+1:t] + pggt) #> //   we can then assign each incremental incidence of dose 1 in deltaT x to getting #> //   second dose in deltaT x+1:t (or not getting it by t) #> // #> // this can be built-up as a matrix once per cohort #> //  1. calculate d2cdf, d2pdf, pgtt as above, for t == max cohort "age" #> //  2. calculate remd2pdf = rev(cumsum(rev(d2pdf))) + pgtt #> //  3. d2contrib = #> //    upper_triangle_1s * colwise element multiplication of d2pdf / rowwise remd2pdf * rowwise d2incidence => column sums => proportion in d2  #>
